docs.content:
--- Page 1 ---
Fleet Command: Strategic Conquest

Game Design Document

Version: 1.0
Date: January 27, 2026
Designer: Mark Gill

Status: Pre-Production

Table of Contents
1. Game Overview
Core Design Pillars

Combat System

YON

Strategic Layer

Technology System
Starting Conditions
Victory Conditions

Technical Implementation

ee ND YY

Teaching Integration

Game Overview

Logline

Build your galactic empire and command diverse fleets in tactical card-based combat against a sophisticated AI

opponent that learns and adapts to your strategies.

Genre

Hybrid 4X Strategy / Deck-Building Card Combat

Target Audience
Â¢ Deck-building enthusiasts (Slay the Spire, Marvel Snap)
e 4X strategy players seeking condensed, tactical gameplay

e Players who want strategic depth without APM requirements


--- Page 2 ---
e Those frustrated by predictable AI in traditional strategy games

Core Gameplay Loop
1. Develop: Capture planets, gather resources, research technologies
2. Build: Construct ships that match your strategic vision
3. Engage: When fleets clash, enter tactical card combat

4. Adapt: AI opponent learns from encounters, forcing strategic evolution

Unique Selling Points
e Ships-as-Deck-Builders: Every vessel contributes specific cards to your combat deck
eÂ¢ AI-Powered Opponent: Claude API integration creates genuinely intelligent decision-making

Â¢ Two-Layer Strategy: Macro empire management feeds into micro tactical combat

Core Design Pillars

1. Meaningful Ship Identity

Every ship matters. Individual vessels have persistence, can be upgraded, and contribute unique capabilities to
your fleet.

2. Tactical Depth Without Complexity

Card-based combat provides strategic decision-making while remaining accessible and teachable.

3. Strategic Consequence

Territory control, resource management, and technological advancement create interconnected systems where
decisions have cascading effects.

4. Intelligent Opposition

Claude-powered AI provides adaptive, learning behavior that feels genuinely strategic rather than scripted.

Combat System

Overview

Combat occurs when opposing fleets occupy the same hex. Players engage in turn-based card combat using

decks constructed from their fleet composition.


--- Page 3 ---
Ship Types
Frigate - Fast Attack / Harassment

e Speed: 4 hexes/turn

Â¢ Role: Mobility, disruption, screening

Â¢ Cost: 1 fleet capacity point

Â¢ Card contributions: Movement, evasion, quick strikes, jamming

e Typical cards: "Evasive Maneuvers", "Torpedo Run", "Sensor Jamming"

Destroyer - Balanced Combat Vessel

Â¢ Speed: 3 hexes/turn

e Role: Flexible response, anti-fighter

Â¢ Cost: 2 fleet capacity points

Â¢ Card contributions: Moderate damage, defensive options, utility

Â¢ Typical cards: "Point Defense", "Tactical Strike", "Shield Modulation"

Battleship - Heavy Assault Platform

e Speed: 2 hexes/turn

Â¢ Role: Line breaker, sustained damage

Â¢ Cost: 3 fleet capacity points

Â¢ Card contributions: High-damage attacks, armor plating, siege weapons

e Typical cards: "Broadside Salvo", "Reinforced Hull", "Main Battery"

Carrier - Force Multiplier

e Speed: 2 hexes/turn

e Role: Fighter deployment, support capabilities

Â¢ Cost: 3 fleet capacity points

Â¢ Card contributions: Fighter swarm cards, buff/debuff effects, repair

e Typical cards: "Launch Fighters", "Fleet Coordination", "Repair Bay"

Dreadnaught - Ultimate Capital Ship

eÂ¢ Speed: 1 hex/turn
e Role: Flagship, game-changer

Â¢ Cost: 5 fleet capacity points


--- Page 4 ---
Â¢ Card contributions: Devastating abilities, high durability, command bonuses

e Typical cards: "Titan Weapon", "Command Authority", "Emergency Shields

eÂ¢ Limit: Maximum | per fleet

Non-Combat Ships

Explorer

Â¢ Speed: 3-4 hexes/turn

e Function: Reveals planet details, plants outposts
Â¢ Cost: Low (Metal + 1-2 turns)

e Sensor range: 4 hexes

Â¢ Incombat: Automatically destroyed if fleet defeated

Colony Ship

e Speed: 1-2 hexes/turn

Â¢ Function: Required to claim/colonize planets
Â¢ Cost: Medium (Metal + Food + 3-4 turns)

e Sensor range: 0 hexes

eÂ¢ JIncombat: Automatically lost if fleet defeated

Deck Composition

Ship Cards:

e Each ship instance contributes its personal card pool to the fleet deck
e Example: A Battleship contributes approximately 8 cards

Â¢ Cards remain in deck even if ship is disabled (become "dead cards")

Fleet Cards:

e Available regardless of ship composition

Â¢ Represent admiral tactics, fleet doctrine, civilization tech
Â¢ Unlocked through research and gameplay

e Always included in battle deck

e Examples: "Coordinated Fire", "Emergency Retreat", "Advanced Targeting"


--- Page 5 ---
Combat Flow

Pre-Battle Phase:

1.

iS)

Fleets meet on galactic map (same hex)

. Player sees enemy fleet composition (via sensor data)

Player selects ships to engage from available fleet
Selected ships' cards + fleet cards form battle deck

Shuffle deck

Turn Structure:

1. Draw to 8 cards (first turn = initial draw, subsequent turns = draw to 8)
2. Play up to 5 cards
3. Resolve immediate effects
4. Enemy turn (same process)
5. Update lasting effects (duration countdown)
6. Begin next turn
Card Types:

Immediate: Resolve instantly (attacks, instant buffs)
Duration: Last X turns (shields, ongoing buffs)
Persistent: Remain until destroyed or dismissed (fighter screens)

Once-per-encounter: Can only be played once per battle

Ship Damage System

Inspired by Star Trek's cascading failure model.

Ship Statistics:

Shield Points (SP): Absorb incoming damage
Hull Points (HP): Permanent until repaired

Damage states based on HP percentage

Damage States:

Operational (100-76% HP)

All systems functional


--- Page 6 ---
e Full combat effectiveness

e All cards available at full strength
Shield Failure (75-51% HP)

e Shields reduced to 50% effectiveness
e Defensive cards: -1 effectiveness

e Visual: Shield flickers

Hull Breach (50-26% HP)

Â¢ Movement cards cost +1 resource or require discarding
Â¢ Movement values: -1

e Visual: Hull sparking, fires visible

Weapons Failure (25-1% HP)

e Attack cards do 50% damage

e Visual: Weapon systems offline, heavy damage

Engine Failure (0% HP)

e Ship disabled, "dead in the water"
Â¢ Cards still in deck/hand but have zero effect when played
Â¢ Cannot be targeted by enemy

Â¢ Visual: Drifting, critical damage

Core Breach (Critical hit or -25% HP)

e Ship destroyed
e Removed from battle

Â¢ Must be rebuilt at shipyard

Damage Resolution Example:

Incoming Damage: 50

Current Shields: 30

Shield absorbs: 30

Hull damage: 20

Result: 180 â€” 160 HP (still Operational)



--- Page 7 ---
Repair System

Damage Control Card:

Each ship has a "Damage Control" card specific to that ship
Effect: "Restore one damage state level (if possible)"
Cannot be played if Operational

May cost discarding another card or resource

Example:

Ship at Weapons Failure (25% HP)

Play "Damage Control"

Ship restored to Hull Breach state (still 25% HP, but weapons functional again)

Shield Regeneration:

Shields can regenerate during combat (card-based or passive per turn)

Hull points do not regenerate during combat

Full repairs occur between encounters

Fleet Composition Rules

Fleet Capacity System:

Starting capacity: 15 points
Upgradeable via technology to 20-25 points
Maximum fleet size: 40-60 ships total owned

Typical battle group: 5-10 ships

Hard Limits:

Maximum | Dreadnaught per fleet
Maximum 2 of any other single ship type

Minimum 3 ships to engage in combat

Example Legal Fleets (15 capacity):

1 Dreadnaught + 2 Battleships + 2 Frigates = 13 points
2 Carriers + 2 Destroyers + 1 Frigate = 11 points

2 Battleships + 2 Destroyers + 2 Frigates = 12 points


--- Page 8 ---
Reinforcement System

Deployment vs Total Fleet:

e =Total Fleet: All ships you own (40-60 maximum)
Â¢ Deployment Capacity: Points you can bring to battle (15-25)
e Engaged Ships: Currently fighting in battle

Reinforcement Mechanics:

e Play reinforcement cards to add ships from deployment reserves
e Reinforcing ships must be within movement range of battle
e Fresh ships enter with full HP/shields

Â¢ Once reserves exhausted, no more reinforcements

Reinforcement Cards:

Â¢ "Call Reinforcements": Add ships from deployment (may require discarding cards)
eÂ« "Emergency Rotation": Swap damaged ship for fresh ship from reserves

e "Fleet Surge": Temporarily exceed deployment cap

Damaged Ship Withdrawal:

e Individual ships can withdraw at end of turn
Â¢ Withdrawn ships remain damaged until all combat ends
Â¢ Cannot return to same battle

e Cards remain in deck as "dead cards"

Withdrawal Mechanics

Standard Withdrawal:
e Available as action at end of turn
Â¢ Can withdraw individual ships or entire fleet
e Lose encounter if entire fleet withdraws

e Ships escape with current damage state

Enemy Interception:

e Enemy can play cards to prevent withdrawal


--- Page 9 ---
e Examples: "Pursuit Course", "Tractor Beam", "Blocking Formation"

e Creates tactical tension and commitment
Victory/Defeat:

e Victory: Enemy fleet destroyed/disabled or enemy withdraws

Â¢ Defeat: Your fleet destroyed/disabled or you withdraw

Strategic Layer

Map Structure
Galactic Map:
e Hex-grid layout
e 12 planets total
Â¢ 2home planets (player + AI, opposite sides)
e 10 neutral/contested planets

Â¢ Some planets have Special resources (3-4 of 12)

Fog of War:

e Planet locations always visible
e Planet details require sensor range or exploration

e Enemy fleet movement invisible unless in sensor range

Resources

Six Resource Types:
Metal

e Purpose: Ship construction, repairs

Â¢ Production: Mining complexes, refineries

Exotics

e Purpose: Advanced technology, special upgrades

e Production: Rare planet deposits, special facilities

Food


--- Page 10 ---
e Purpose: Fleet maintenance, population growth

e Production: Agricultural planets, hydroponic facilities

Energy

Â¢ Purpose: Operations, shields, general upkeep

Â¢ Production: Power plants, stellar collectors

Commerce

e Purpose: Economy, trade, research funding

e Production: Trade ports, commerce centers

Special

e Purpose: Unique planetary bonuses

e Production: Cannot be produced, only found on specific planets

Resource Management:

e Galactic stockpile (all resources pooled empire-wide)
e Planets contribute resources per turn to stockpile
e Captured planets stop contributing until reclaimed

Â¢ Resource depletion can halt production

Planet Development

Planet Information Tiers:
Tier 1: Distant Scan (any sensor range)

e Reveals: Metal, Food, Energy production values

e Reveals: Enemy presence if occupied

Tier 2: Explorer Survey (explorer in same hex)

e Reveals: Exotics, Commerce, Special resources
e Reveals: Detailed enemy improvements

e Action: Can plant outpost

Tier 3: Colonization (colony ship in same hex)


--- Page 11 ---
Â¢ Claims planet for empire
Â¢ Begins resource contribution

e Enables improvement construction

Planet Improvements:
Shipyard (3 levels)

e Function: Construct ships

e Level 1: 1 construction queue
e Level 2: 2 simultaneous queues
e Level 3: 3 simultaneous queues

Â¢ Cost: Metal + Energy + 3-5 turns

Research Lab

e Function: Required for technology research
eÂ¢ Multiple labs enable higher tier research

e Cost: Metal + Exotics + 3-5 turns

Mining Complex / Refinery (3 levels)

Â¢ Function: Increase Metal/Exotics production
e Each level: +X per turn

e Cost: Metal + 3-5 turns

Defense Grid (3 levels)

e Level 1: Orbital platforms (2 frigate equivalent)

e Level 2: Siege cannons (5 ship equivalent)

e Level 3: Starbase (10+ ship equivalent)

Â¢ Note: Dreadnaught can break any defense (takes time/damage)

Â¢ Cost: Metal + Energy + 5-7 turns

Spaceport

Â¢ Function: Reinforcement staging, repair capacity
e Enables faster fleet organization

e Cost: Metal + Commerce + 3-4 turns


--- Page 12 ---
Commerce Center (3 levels)

Â¢ Function: Increase Commerce production
e Enables trade routes (future feature)

Â¢ Cost: Commerce + 3-5 turns

Special Facility

Â¢ Function: Unlock planet-specific bonuses
e Requires: Special resource on planet
eÂ« Examples:
e "Precursor Forge": Fleet in system +2 speed

e "Psionic Nexus": +1 card draw in combat for local fleets

Â¢ Cost: Exotics + Special + 5-10 turns

Capture Mechanics:
eÂ¢ When planet captured, all improvements destroyed
Â¢ Must rebuild from scratch

Â¢ Creates high stakes for defending developed planets

Sensor System

Sensor Ranges:

e Explorer: 4 hexes
Â¢ Combat ships: 2 hexes
Â¢ Colony Ship: 0 hexes

Â¢ Outposts: 1 hex radius around planet

Information Revealed in Sensor Range:

e Planet: Basic resources (Metal/Food/Energy) + enemy presence
e Enemy Fleet: Ship count, ship types, estimated battle capacity

Â¢ Movement: Real-time tracking while in range

Outpost System:

e Planted by Explorer when visiting planet

e Provides: 1-hex vision radius around planet


--- Page 13 ---
e Vulnerable: Destroyed instantly when enemy enters hex
e Visible: Can be seen by enemy sensors

Â¢ No limit on number of outposts

Movement and Battle Groups
Battle Groups:
e Minimum size: 1 ship
Â¢ Move together at slowest ship's speed

eÂ¢ Example: Group with Dreadnaught moves | hex/turn regardless of other ships

Ship Speeds:
e Frigate: 4 hexes/turn
Â¢ Destroyer: 3 hexes/turn
Â¢ Battleship/Carrier: 2 hexes/turn
e Dreadnaught: 1 hex/turn
e Explorer: 3-4 hexes/turn

Â¢ Colony Ship: 1-2 hexes/turn

Combat Initiation:

Â¢ Occurs when two opposing fleets occupy same hex

Â¢ Automatic engagement (diplomacy is roadmap feature)

Reinforcement Range:

e Battle groups within their speed distance can reinforce
eÂ¢ Example: Battle at Hex A, group 3 hexes away with speed 3 = can reinforce

Â¢ Must use reinforcement cards during combat to call them in

Turn Structure
Strategic Turn Phases:
1. Income Phase: Collect resources from planets
2. Orders Phase:
Â¢ Move battle groups

e Start construction projects


--- Page 14 ---
Â¢ Begin research
Â¢ Upgrade planets
Â¢ Organize fleets
3. Resolution Phase:
Â¢ Complete construction timers
e Finish research projects
e Trigger combat encounters
4. Next Turn

Construction Times:

e Frigate: 2 turns

Â¢ Destroyer: 3 turns

e Battleship: 5 turns

e Carrier: 5 turns

e Dreadnaught: 10 turns
e Explorer: 1-2 turns

Â¢ Colony Ship: 3-4 turns

e Planet improvements: 3-7 turns (varies by type and level)

Technology System

Overview

Four technology trees representing different strategic paths. Research speed influenced by starting condition
choice. Labs can be allocated flexibly to pursue multiple research goals.

Tech Trees
Industry Tree Focus: Production, efficiency, economy
Tier 1: Advanced Manufacturing

e Effect: Reduce ship construction time 25%

e Cost: 5 turns with 1 lab

Tier 2: Resource Extraction

e Effect: +1 Metal/Exotics production per planet


--- Page 15 ---
e Cost: 10 turns with 2 labs

Tier 3: Efficient Construction

e Effect: Planet improvements build 30% faster

eÂ¢ Cost: 15 turns with 3 labs

Tier 4: Mass Production

e Effect: Shipyards build 2 ships simultaneously per queue

Â¢ Cost: 20 turns with 4 labs

Tier 5: Industrial Might

e Effect: All production costs -20%

Â¢ Cost: 25 turns with 5 labs

Higher tiers: Automated factories, mega-structures
Exploration Tree Focus: Sensors, speed, colonization, discovery
Tier 1: Enhanced Sensors

e Effect: All ships +1 sensor range

Â¢ Cost: 5 turns with 1 lab

Tier 2: Advanced Propulsion

e =Â©Effect: All ships +1 movement speed

Â¢ Cost: 10 turns with 2 labs

Tier 3: Gas Giant Colonization

e Effect: Unlock colonizing exotic planet types

Â¢ Cost: 15 turns with 3 labs

Tier 4: Deep Space Sensors

e Effect: Reveal all planet resources (removes need for exploration)

Â¢ Cost: 20 turns with 4 labs

Tier 5: Warp Navigation

e Effect: Movement ignores terrain penalties (if implemented)


--- Page 16 ---
e Cost: 25 turns with 5 labs

Tier 6: Galactic Cartography

e Effect: Full map reveal, permanent

Â¢ Cost: 30 turns with 6 labs

Military Tree Focus: Weapons, fleet size, ship mastery
Tier 1: Weapon Systems

e Effect: All attack cards +1 damage

Â¢ Cost: 5 turns with 1 lab

Tier 2: Fleet Expansion

e Effect: Deployment capacity +5 points

Â¢ Cost: 10 turns with 2 labs

Tier 3: Advanced Armor

e Effect: All ships +20 HP

e Cost: 15 turns with 3 labs

Tier 4: Dreadnaught Construction

e =Effect: Unlock building Dreadnaughts

Â¢ Cost: 20 turns with 4 labs

Tier 5: Ship Mastery I
e Effect: Unlock leveling ships to level 6
Â¢ Cost: 25 turns with 5 labs

Tier 6: Ship Mastery IT
e Effect: Unlock leveling ships to level 8
Â¢ Cost: 30 turns with 6 labs

Tier 7: Ultimate Weapons

e Effect: Unlock leveling ships to level 10

Â¢ Cost: 35 turns with 7 labs


--- Page 17 ---
Humanity Tree Focus: Crew abilities, survival, psionics, transcendence
Tier 1: Survival Training

e Effect: Food requirements -25%

Â¢ Cost: 5 turns with 1 lab

Tier 2: Genetic Enhancement

e Effect: Repair cards 25% stronger

Â¢ Cost: 10 turns with 2 labs

Tier 3: Meditation Protocols

e Effect: Unlock "Morale" category fleet cards

e Cost: 15 turns with 3 labs

Tier 4: Psionic Awakening

e Effect: Unlock ESP-based cards ("Precognition", "Mind Shield")

Â¢ Cost: 20 turns with 4 labs

Tier 5: Transcendence

e Effect: Unlock powerful humanity abilities

eÂ¢ Cost: 25 turns with 5 labs

Tier 6: Collective Consciousness

e Effect: Fleet-wide buffs, coordination bonuses

Â¢ Cost: 30 turns with 6 labs

Research Mechanics

Lab Requirements:
e Tier 1: Requires 1 research lab empire-wide
e Tier 2: Requires 2 research labs empire-wide
Â¢ Tier 3: Requires 3 research labs empire-wide

e And so on...

Lab Allocation:


--- Page 18 ---
e Labs can be allocated flexibly to research goals
Â¢ Multiple tier 1 techs can be researched simultaneously
e Higher tier techs require combining multiple labs
Â¢ Example with 4 labs:
e Research | tier 4 tech (all 4 labs)
e Research 2 tier 2 techs (2+2 labs)
e Research | tier 3 + 1 tier 1 (3+1 labs)

e Research 4 tier 1 techs (1+1+1+1 labs)

Research Time:

e Base: Tier 5 turns

e = Tier 1: 5 turns (with 1 lab)

e Tier 2: 10 turns (with 2 labs)
e Tier 3: 15 turns (with 3 labs)
e Tier 4: 20 turns (with 4 labs)

Starting Condition Modifiers:

Â¢ Bonus tree (matching start): 1.0x research time (normal)
e Neutral trees: 1.5x research time (slower)

Â¢ Opposed tree: 2.0x research time (much slower)

Starting Bonuses by Choice:

e Industrial Start: Industry 1.0x, others 1.5x, Military 2.0x
e Expansion Start: Exploration 1.0x, others 1.5x, Military 2.0x (example)
Â¢ Military Start: Military 1.0x, others 1.5x, Industry 2.0x

e Balanced Start: Humanity 1.0x, others 1.5x

Research Queue System:

e Can cancel and reallocate labs mid-research

e Progress is saved/banked when cancelled

Â¢ Cancelled tech enters queue

e Resumes automatically when labs become available

Â¢ Multiple techs can be queued


--- Page 19 ---
Prerequisites:

e Must unlock tiers in order (cannot skip)
Â¢ No branching within a tree (linear progression)

Â¢ Can pursue multiple trees simultaneously

Ship Leveling System
Default Levels:
e All ships start at Level 1

Â¢ Can level up to Level 4 without technology

e Levels 5+ require Military tree research

Level Unlocks:

e Levels 1-4: Available from start
e Levels 5-6: Requires Ship Mastery I (Military Tier 5)
Â¢ Levels 7-8: Requires Ship Mastery II (Military Tier 6)

e Levels 9-10: Requires Ultimate Weapons (Military Tier 7)

Benefits Per Level:

e +20 HP per level

e +10 Shield capacity per level
e Enhanced baseline stats (damage, defense, movement)

Â¢ Unlock additional card variants for that ship (future feature)

Leveling Mechanics:

e Ships gain experience from surviving battles (future feature)
Â¢ Manual upgrades at shipyards (costs resources + time)

e Maximum level determined by unlocked technology

Starting Conditions

Players choose one of four starting conditions that shape their early game strategy and provide research

bonuses.


--- Page 20 ---
Option 1: Expansion Start
Assets:
e 1 Explorer
eÂ¢ 1 Colony Ship
Â¢ 3 Frigates
Â¢ Home planet (no improvements)

Â¢ Low resource stockpile

Tech Bonus:

e Exploration tree: 1.0x research speed

e Other trees: 1.5x research speed

Strategy:

Â¢ Rush nearby planets
e Establish territory quickly

Â¢ Convert space control into economic advantage

Risk:

Â¢ Weak home defense
e Vulnerable to early military pressure

e Must establish colonies before benefits kick in

Option 2: Industrial Start

Assets:

e 1 Explorer
Â¢ Home planet with: Level | Shipyard, Level | Refinery, Level 1 Research Lab

e High resource stockpile

Tech Bonus:

e Industry tree: 1.0x research speed

e Other trees: 1.5x research speed

Strategy:


--- Page 21 ---
e Build strong economic foundation
e Rapid mid-game fleet production

Â¢ Outproduce opponent over time

Risk:
e Slow initial expansion

e AI may claim key planets first

Â¢Â« Must survive early game without military strength

Option 3: Military Start
Assets:
e 1 Explorer
e 2 Destroyers
e 4 Frigates
Â¢ Home planet with: Level | Shipyard

e Low resource stockpile

Tech Bonus:

e Military tree: 1.0x research speed

e Other trees: 1.5x research speed

Strategy:

e Aggressive early game
Â¢ Contest planets with force

Â¢ Pressure opponent before they develop

Risk:

Â¢ No immediate colonization capability
Â¢ Must build colony ship(s)

e Resource constraints limit sustained aggression

Option 4: Balanced Start

Assets:


--- Page 22 ---
e 1 Explorer

Â¢ 1 Colony Ship

e 2 Frigates

Â¢ Home planet with: Level 1 Shipyard

Â¢ Medium resource stockpile

Tech Bonus:

Â¢Â« Humanity tree: 1.0x research speed

e Other trees: 1.5x research speed

Strategy:

e Flexible opening
e Adapt to map and opponent

e Access to unique Humanity techs

Risk:
Â¢ Not exceptional at any specific strategy
e Jack of all trades, master of none

Â¢ Requires good decision-making to leverage flexibility

Victory Conditions

Primary Victory: Elimination

Defeat all enemy factions. Game ends when no hostile forces remain.

Enemy Surrender Conditions

AI will surrender when:

Â¢ No shipyard capacity: All shipyards destroyed, cannot build more
Â¢ Resource depletion: Insufficient resources to maintain operations + no capacity to generate more
Â¢ Home planet captured: Morale break, forced surrender

Â¢ Overwhelming disadvantage: Fleet strength disparity beyond recovery threshold (Claude AI decision)


--- Page 23 ---
Alternative Victory Path: Diplomatic Peace
Â¢ Roadmap feature: Diplomacy system
e Negotiate peace terms

e Win through alliance or vassalization

Defeat Conditions

Player loses when:

eÂ¢ Home planet captured
e All shipyards destroyed + no resources to rebuild
e All ships destroyed + no production capacity

e Voluntary surrender

Game Length Estimates
e Short game: 30-50 turns
Â¢ Medium game: 50-80 turns

e Long game: 80-120 turns

Factors affecting length:

e Starting condition choices
e Aggression level
Â¢ Map control dynamics

e Technology race outcomes

Technical Implementation
Architecture Overview
Three Major Systems:

1. Galactic Layer: Strategic map, planets, resources, tech tree
2. Fleet Management: Ship composition, deck building, upgrades

3. Card Combat: Tactical battles using constructed decks

Unity Structure


--- Page 24 ---
Core Systems:

e GameManager: Overall game state, turn management
Â¢ ResourceManager: Galactic resource stockpile tracking
e FleetManager: All fleet compositions, battle groups

eÂ¢ CombatManager: Battle resolution, card play logic

e TechManager: Research progress, unlocks

e AIManager: Claude API integration for opponent decisions

Data Structures:

esharp



--- Page 25 ---
public class Ship {
public string shipName;

public ShipType type;

=]

public int currentHP;
public int maxHP;

public int currentShields;
public int maxShields;

public int level;

public List<Card> cardPool;

public DamageState damageState;

public int speed;

public class Planet {
public string planetName;
public Vector2Int gridPosition;
public PlanetOwner owner;
public Dictionary<ResourceType, int> resources;
public List<Improvement> improvements;

public bool hasSpecialResource;

public string specialResourceType;

public class Card {
public string cardName;
public CardType type;
public string description;
public int cost;
public CardEffect[] effects;
public Ship sourceShip;

public bool oncePerEncounter;

Claude API Integration

Purpose: AI opponent for both strategic and tactical decision-making
Implementation Approach:

1. Game State Serialization: Serialize current game state to JSON at decision points:



--- Page 26 ---
json

Â§
v

"strategic_state": {
"turn": 15,
"ai_resources": {"metal": 500, "energy": 300},
"ai planets": [...],
"player_visible_ info": {...},

mow

}
}
X
json
{
"combat_state": {
"ai fleet": {
"ships": [...],
"available cards": [...],
"current_hand": [...]
Js

"valid_plays": [...]
}
5
X

2. API Request Structure:

esharp



--- Page 27 ---
public async Task<AIDecision> GetAIDecision(GameState state) {
var request = new {
model = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
max_tokens = 1000,
messages = new[] {
new {
role = "user",

content = $"Analyze this game state and return your decision as JSON: {JsonUtility.ToJson(state)}"

var response = await SendAPIRequest(request);

return ParseA[Decision(response);

3. Decision Tiers:

e Strategic Layer: Tech choices, planet development, fleet movements (slower decisions)

Â¢ Tactical Layer: Card play during combat (faster responses required)

4. Prompt Engineering: System prompts guide AI behavior:

e Strategic: "You are a space fleet admiral managing an empire..."
Â¢ Tactical: "You are commanding a fleet in combat. Analyze available cards..."

e Include difficulty settings via prompts (aggressive, defensive, balanced)

Student Learning Outcomes

Week-by-Week Implementation Plan:
Weeks 1-2: Data structures

e Ship definitions, card definitions, tech tree structures
e JSON serialization practice

Â¢ Unity ScriptableObjects for data

Weeks 3-4: Card combat system



--- Page 28 ---
e Turn management
eÂ¢ Hand management (draw, discard)
e Card effect resolution

e Ship damage calculations

Weeks 5-6: Fleet management UI
e Deck composition interface

e Ship selection for battles

e Fleet organization

Weeks 7-8: Galactic map and movement
Â¢ Hex grid pathfinding
Â¢ Turn-based movement

Â¢ Combat encounter triggers

Weeks 9-10: AI integration

e API authentication and requests
Â¢ Game state serialization
Â¢ Response parsing

e Error handling and fallbacks

Weeks 11-12: Tech tree and progression

e Research system implementation
e Tech unlock conditions

Â¢ UI for tech selection

Weeks 13-14: Polish and balance

e =Â©Playtesting
Â¢ Balance adjustments

e Bug fixes

Technical Challenges
Challenge 1: API Rate Limiting


--- Page 29 ---
Solution: Cache AI decisions, implement local fallback behaviors

Show students cost monitoring and optimization

Challenge 2: Asyne Operations

Solution: Unity coroutines and async/await patterns

Teach proper UI feedback during network operations

Challenge 3: Game State Complexity

Solution: Clean serialization, modular state representation

Demonstrates importance of data architecture

Challenge 4: Balance Testing

Solution: Iterative playtesting, data-driven balance values

Students learn game balance methodology

Teaching Integration

Pedagogical Goals

Primary Objectives:

I.

w

Demonstrate professional game design documentation
Teach modular system architecture

Show API integration in game contexts

Illustrate iterative design process

Provide complex project for portfolio development

Design Document as Teaching Tool

This document serves multiple purposes:

Reference: Students can consult during implementation
Model: Demonstrates professional documentation standards
Living Document: Updates as design evolves through testing

Communication: Clear specifications prevent misunderstandings


--- Page 30 ---
Discussion Topics

Game Design:

e Why card combat over real-time space combat?
Â¢ How do the four starting conditions create different strategies?
Â¢ What makes ship identity meaningful vs ships as generic units?

e Why hex grids for strategy games?

System Design:

Â¢ How do resources flow through the economy?
eÂ¢ What creates interesting decision-making in the tech tree?
eÂ¢ Why allow research queue flexibility vs commitment penalties?

Â¢ How does fog of war create strategic value?

AI Design:

eÂ« Why use Claude API instead of traditional game AI?

Appendix A: Card Examples
Sample Ship Cards:

"Broadside Salvo" (Battleship)

Â¢ Type: Immediate Attack


--- Page 32 ---
e Effect: Deal 30 damage to target ship. If this ship is Operational, deal 40 damage instead.

e Flavor: "All batteries, fire at will."

"Evasive Maneuvers" (Frigate)

e Type: Duration (2 turns)
e Effect: This ship gains +2 to evasion. Enemy attack cards targeting this ship have -10 damage.

e Flavor: "Can't hit what you can't catch."

"Launch Fighters" (Carrier)

e =6Type: Persistent

Â¢ Effect: Deploy fighter screen. All enemy attacks reduced by 5 damage until fighter screen is destroyed.
Fighter screen has 30 HP.

e Flavor: "Scramble all squadrons."

"Damage Control" (Universal - each ship has one)

Â¢ Type: Immediate Repair
e Effect: Restore this ship one damage state level. Discard one card. Cannot be played if Operational.

e Flavor: "Engineering teams reporting."

Sample Fleet Cards:
"Coordinated Fire"

e Type: Immediate Buff

e Effect: Choose up to 3 attack cards in your hand. They gain +5 damage this turn. Requires at least 2

operational ships.

e Flavor: "Focus fire on their flagship!"

"Emergency Withdrawal"

e Type: Immediate Tactical

e Effect: Choose one of your ships. It immediately withdraws from combat. That ship cannot return this

battle.

e Flavor: "Fall back! Preserve the ship!"

"Advanced Targeting"

e Type: Duration (3 turns)


--- Page 33 ---
e Effect: All your attack cards ignore 10 shield points.

e Flavor: "Target their subsystems directly."
"Fleet Morale" (Humanity tech unlock)

e Type: Duration (2 turns)
e Effect: All your ships gain +1 to all card effects. Draw | additional card next turn.

e Flavor: "For humanity! For the stars!"

Appendix B: Technology Dependencies
Example Tech Paths:

Path 1: Dreadnaught Rush

1. Military T1: Weapon Systems (5 turns, 1 lab)

2. Build second research lab (colonize planet, 5 turns)

3. Military T2: Fleet Expansion (10 turns, 2 labs)

4. Build third and fourth labs (15+ turns total)

5. Military T3: Advanced Armor (15 turns, 3 labs)

6. Military T4: Dreadnaught Construction (20 turns, 4 labs)

Total: ~60+ turns minimum

Path 2: Economic Expansion

1. Industry T1: Advanced Manufacturing (5 turns, | lab)

ad

Exploration T1: Enhanced Sensors (5 turns, | lab)

3. Build second lab, expand to 3 planets (20 turns)

4. Industry T2: Resource Extraction (10 turns, 2 labs)
5. Build third lab (5 turns)

6. Industry T3: Efficient Construction (15 turns, 3 labs)

Total: ~40+ turns for strong economy

Path 3: Psionic Powers

1. Humanity T1: Survival Training (5 turns, 1 lab)
2. Build second lab (5+ turns)

3. Humanity T2: Genetic Enhancement (10 turns, 2 labs)


--- Page 34 ---
4. Build third and fourth labs (15+ turns)
5. Humanity T3: Meditation Protocols (15 turns, 3 labs)

6. Humanity T4: Psionic Awakening (20 turns, 4 labs)

Â¢ Total: ~70+ turns for ESP powers

Appendix C: Balance Considerations

Resource Production Rates:

e Early game planet: 10-15 resources/turn per type
e Mid game (with improvements): 25-40 resources/turn

e Late game (fully developed): 50-80 resources/turn

Ship Costs (Example):
e Frigate: 50 Metal, 20 Energy
Â¢ Destroyer: 100 Metal, 40 Energy
e Battleship: 200 Metal, 80 Energy, 20 Exotics
Â¢ Carrier: 200 Metal, 60 Energy, 40 Exotics
e Dreadnaught: 500 Metal, 200 Energy, 100 Exotics

Improvement Costs (Example):

e Research Lab: 100 Metal, 50 Exotics

e Shipyard L1: 150 Metal, 50 Energy

Â¢ Defense Grid L1: 100 Metal, 80 Energy

Â¢ Mining Complex L1: 80 Metal, 40 Energy

Combat Balance:

e Average combat: 5-10 turns
e Hand of 8 cards, play up to 5 = good strategic decisions

e Ship HP ranges: Frigate 100, Destroyer 150, Battleship 250, Carrier 200, Dreadnaught 400

**Risk Register**

Known risks and mitigation strategies for the semester:

  ------------------- ------------ ----------------------------------------
  **Risk**            **Impact**   **Mitigation**

  Scope creep ---     High         Stick to GDD. Any new features go on a
  adding features                  backlog list and are only considered
  beyond GDD                       after Week 9 is complete.

  Claude API rate     Medium       Fallback AI (rule-based) must be
  limits or downtime               implemented in Week 7. Game must be
                                   playable without API.

  Unity version       Medium       Stay on Unity 6 URP throughout. Do not
  incompatibilities                upgrade mid-project.

  Codex misinterprets Low          Always review the plan before
  a prompt                         green-lighting. Never skip the plan
                                   review step.

  Week 1-2 bugs eat   High         Buffer built into Weeks 3-4 --- combat
  into later weeks                 core has room to absorb 2-3 extra debug
                                   days if needed.

  Save system         Medium       Week 8 has save system as its sole
  complexity                       complex task --- enough dedicated time
  underestimated                   to handle it properly.

  Final build fails   High         Week 10 Day 29 is dedicated entirely to
  or crashes                       build testing --- 1 full day buffer
                                   before submission.
  ------------------- ------------ ----------------------------------------

**Must-Have Delivery Checklist**

These are the non-negotiable items for instructor submission:

-   **✅ Full combat system --- cards, damage, turn loop,
    victory/defeat**

-   **✅ All 5 ship types --- Frigate, Destroyer, Battleship, Carrier,
    Dreadnaught with unique card pools**

-   **✅ Working AI opponent --- Claude API integration with fallback
    for offline**

-   **✅ Full tech tree --- all 4 trees, lab allocation, unlock effects
    wired**

-   **✅ Save/load system --- full game state persisted to JSON**

-   Galactic map with planets, resources, fog of war

-   Main menu and scene transitions

-   UI polish across all panels

-   Standalone PC build

-   Updated GDD/SDD documentation

*Fleet Command --- Built by Mario + Codex + Claude*
